pattern: Counting each character's contribution as the number of substrings in which it is the most recent occurrence of its letter collapses the double sum over substrings into one linear pass.
hints: Instead of scoring whole substrings, ask how much each single position contributes to the grand total.
A character at index i is the freshest copy of its letter for every substring that starts after that letter's previous occurrence and ends at or beyond i.
Track the last index where each letter appeared; at position i the new contribution is (i - last[c]) times the number of substrings ending here, accumulated into a running total.

edge_inputs.description: letter recurs only at the ends

stress.description: long random string over a small alphabet to maximise recurrences
stress.note: small alphabet forces frequent last-occurrence updates
